narratives.evidence_quote: दुनिया जंग के ढेर पर बैठी है. एक दर्जन से ज्यादा देशों में तनाव है... कुल मिलाकर दुनिया बारूद के ढेर पर बैठी है.
narratives.reasoning: The text uses dramatic, fear-inducing language ('sitting on a heap of war', 'sitting on a heap of gunpowder') to evoke anxiety about widespread global conflict and tension in over a dozen countries.

narratives.evidence_quote: उससे लग रहा है कि इजरायल, मिडिल ईस्ट का नया बादशाह बन सकता है.
narratives.reasoning: The text makes a speculative prediction about a potential future outcome of the conflict, suggesting Israel could become the 'new emperor' of the Middle East.

narratives.evidence_quote: इसके बाद लेबनान और इज़रायल में बड़ी जंग छिड़ने के आसार हैं.
narratives.reasoning: The text makes an assumption about a potential future consequence, predicting the prospects of a major war breaking out between Lebanon and Israel.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: Russia will also attack other countries
subnarratives.evidence_quote: नाटो की फौजें रूस को चुनौती दे रही हैं.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement frames NATO forces as actively challenging Russia, implying a direct military confrontation between Russia and the NATO alliance, which consists of multiple other countries. This promotes the fear that Russia's conflict could expand to involve attacks on other nations.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: दुनिया जंग के ढेर पर बैठी है... कुल मिलाकर दुनिया बारूद के ढेर पर बैठी है.
subnarratives.reasoning: This repeated metaphor of the world sitting on a heap of gunpowder/war actively amplifies generalized, catastrophic war-related fears. It supports the parent narrative of amplifying war-related fears but does not fit the specific definitions of WW3, nuclear weapons, or NATO intervention as the primary cause.
